originalTitle: Krafton settles with Subnautica 2 developer after drawn-out dispute over $250 million
summary: After a lengthy legal dispute, Krafton has settled with its subsidiary Unknown Worlds Entertainment, which is developing Subnautica 2, and will pay bonuses to the studio's staff, Bloomberg reports. The dispute began last year after Krafton pushed out Unknown Worlds' cofounders, Charlie Cleveland and Max McGuire, and its CEO, Ted Gill, ahead of a potential […]
content: After a lengthy legal dispute, Krafton has settled with its subsidiary Unknown Worlds Entertainment, which is developing Subnautica 2, and will pay bonuses to the studio’s staff, Bloomberg reports. The dispute began last year after Krafton pushed out Unknown Worlds’ cofounders, Charlie Cleveland and Max McGuire, and its CEO, Ted Gill, ahead of a potential $250 million bonus for hitting certain financial goals that would have been shared with the studio. After the executives sued, a judge reinstated Gill as CEO in March, and Subnautica 2 finally launched in early access in May. The game passed four million copies sold in five days. Details of the settlement haven’t been disclosed. “Krafton, Inc., Unknown Worlds Entertainment, and Ted Gill, Charlie Cleveland, and Max McGuire have reached a mutual settlement and agreed to dismiss all pending legal proceedings,” Krafton said in a statement to Game Developer. Bonuses will be paid to all Unknown Worlds staffers instead of only those who were there when Krafton acquired the studio in 2021, and they’ll be paid out in three yearly installments, according to Bloomberg. However, Gill will be leaving the studio. “We mutually agreed to part ways,” Gill tells Bloomberg. “New leadership is the best way for the studio to move forward.” Krafton didn’t immediately reply to a request for comment from The Verge.
chineseTitle: Krafton 与 Subnautica 2 开发商就 2.5 亿美元旷日持久的纠纷达成和解
chineseSummary: 据彭博社报道，经过漫长的法律纠纷，Krafton 已与其正在开发《深海迷航 2》的子公司 Unknown Worlds Entertainment 达成和解，并将向工作室员工支付奖金。这场争议始于去年，当时克拉夫顿将未知世界的联合创始人查理·克利夫兰和马克斯·麦奎尔及其首席执行官特德·吉尔赶出了潜在的[…]
chineseContent: 据彭博社报道，经过漫长的法律纠纷，Krafton 已与其正在开发《深海迷航 2》的子公司 Unknown Worlds Entertainment 达成和解，并将向工作室员工支付奖金。这场纠纷始于去年，当时克拉夫顿驱逐了未知世界的联合创始人查理·克利夫兰和马克斯·麦奎尔及其首席执行官特德·吉尔，因为达成某些财务目标可能会与工作室分享2.5亿美元的奖金。在高管提起诉讼后，法官于 3 月份恢复了吉尔的首席执行官职务，《深海迷航 2》最终于 5 月份推出了抢先体验版。该游戏在五天内售出了 400 万份。和解的细节尚未披露。 “Krafton, Inc.、Unknown Worlds Entertainment、Ted Gill、Charlie Cleveland 和 Max McGuire 已达成共同和解，并同意驳回所有悬而未决的法律诉讼，”Krafton 在给游戏开发商的一份声明中表示。据彭博社报道，奖金将支付给《未知世界》的所有员工，而不仅仅是 2021 年 Krafton 收购该工作室时在场的员工，而且奖金将每年分三年支付。然而，吉尔将离开工作室。 “我们一致同意分道扬镳，”吉尔告诉彭博社。 “新的领导层是工作室前进的最佳方式。”克拉夫顿没有立即回复 The Verge 的置评请求。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
